next_steps: Continuous video-EEG monitoring to differentiate ictal/postictal states from psychiatric symptoms
Comprehensive autoimmune encephalopathy antibody panel (serum and CSF)
Lumbar puncture for CSF analysis to rule out infectious or inflammatory processes
Multidisciplinary review by epilepsy surgery team
Pharmacological optimization balancing seizure control with psychiatric safety
High-acuity psychiatric consultation for management of persistent agitation

differential_diagnoses: Antiepileptic medication-induced encephalopathy or psychiatric adverse effects
Delirium secondary to metabolic or pharmacologic disturbances
Interictal dysphoric disorder with comorbid psychosis
next_steps: Check serum concentrations of all antiepileptic drugs to assess for toxicity or therapeutic sub-levels
Perform a metabolic and electrolyte panel to rule out contributions to acute delirium
Review current medication regimen and cautiously reduce or rotate offending agents (e.g., topiramate, rapid dosage escalations)
Continue intensive video-EEG monitoring to confirm if psychiatric episodes are temporally associated with electrographic seizures
Consult psychiatry for management of severe agitation and to perform a mental status exam independent of seizure activity
Consider an autoimmune encephalitis antibody panel if symptoms fail to resolve with seizure stabilization
Optimize long-term epilepsy management plan, potentially evaluating for surgical intervention given the confirmed left mesial temporal focus
